response:
Yes we can check how long it took for udp packet to be sent to server like ping but only one direction

To do this we can use a udp sender and measure the time between sending the packet and receiving the response

We can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient class to send and receive packets

We can measure the time difference between sending and receiving the packet

We can use the System.Diagnostics.Stopwatch class to measure the time

We can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient.Receive method to receive the response

We can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient.Send method to send the packet

We can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient.Client property to get the client

We can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient.RemoteEndPoint property to get the remote endpoint

We can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient.ReceiveBufferSize property to get the receive buffer size

We can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient.SendBufferSize property to get the send buffer size

We can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient.ReceiveTimeout property to set the receive timeout

We can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient.SendTimeout property to set the send timeout

We can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient.ReceiveBufferSize property to get the receive buffer size

We can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient.SendBufferSize property to get the send buffer size

We can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient.ReceiveTimeout property to set the receive timeout

We can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient.SendTimeout property to set the send timeout

We can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient.ReceiveBufferSize property to get the receive buffer size

We can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient.SendBufferSize property to get the send buffer size

We can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient.ReceiveTimeout property to set the receive timeout

We can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient.SendTimeout property to set the send timeout

We can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient.ReceiveBufferSize property to get the receive buffer size

We can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient.SendBufferSize property to get the send buffer size

We can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient.ReceiveTimeout property to set the receive timeout

We can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient.SendTimeout property to set the send timeout

We can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient.ReceiveBufferSize property to get the receive buffer size

We can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient.SendBufferSize property to get the send buffer size
